text.title: structured-tool-errors Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for structured-tool-errors. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Every tool error response MUST be a JSON object with these required fields: code (UPPER_SNAKE machine code), message (one human sentence, no stack trace), recoveryHint (one of RETRY_LATER | CHECK_INPUT | TRY_ALTERNATIVE | REPORT_TO_USER | NEEDS_AUTH), traceId (correlation ID). Optional: retry_after_seconds, details (sanitised, no stack frames).
rule.rationale: SHIELDA and Kumaran's MCP error-design study measured a 30%+ drop in dead-end retry loops when tools emit a closed recoveryHint enum vs free-form messages. The closed enum is what makes the policy mapping deterministic.

rule.statement: The agent runner MUST map each recoveryHint value to exactly one action: RETRY_LATER → wait retry_after_seconds (clamped 1-300s) and retry the same call up to a per-tool retry budget; CHECK_INPUT → return the error to the model with no automatic retry; TRY_ALTERNATIVE → return to the model, signal to pick a different tool; REPORT_TO_USER → halt the loop, surface to user; NEEDS_AUTH → halt the loop, request credentials.
rule.rationale: Without an explicit mapping, the model invents its own retry policy each turn, often retrying INVALID_ARGUMENT (waste) or escalating RATE_LIMITED (premature). Explicit dispatch is cheaper and safer.
